title: Pharmacy High Risk Med Release Checks
description: A hospital-operations and compliance workflow that enforces control checks while allocating operational resources and closing governance loops. Focus case: pharmacy high risk med release checks.
assumptions: Each high_risk_release_task (T1) represents a single patient-specific medication release requiring multiple discrete checks.
Resources that are reserved (drug_lot, scanning_terminal, dispensing_location) are exclusive while reserved and must be explicitly released.
Secondary checks and witness signatures are required for defined combinations of medication_order, administration_protocol and drug_lot.
Quality documents and authorization documents are authoritative and must be linked to the task for auditability before final signoff.
risks: Resource contention: multiple tasks competing for the same drug_lot or scanning_terminal could delay releases.
Role ambiguity: reusing the same human-identifier for distinct staff roles risks violating segregation of duties.
Device misuse: improper assignment of scanning terminals to unrelated checks could produce invalid verification evidence.
Audit gaps: failure to attach or persist quality_document or authorization_document will compromise compliance evidence.
